Gage Reschke
Project — Jan 2024 to Apr 2024

Water Runoff
Sustainability
Pinball Machine

CourseCornerstone of Engineering
RoleProject Lead
TimelineJan – Apr 2024
ToolsCAD, Arduino, Machine Shop

Overview

For our Cornerstone of Engineering project, our team designed, built, and presented a fully functional pinball machine themed around water runoff and sustainability. The machine was built to educate players on how rainfall travels through urban and natural environments — and how pollutants enter waterways.

I led the project from initial concept through final presentation, coordinating design decisions, fabrication scheduling, and system integration across the team.

Mechanical Design & Fabrication

The enclosure and playfield were designed in CAD and fabricated using laser-cut plywood and machine shop tools. Key mechanical challenges included:

  • Designing and fabricating dynamic magnetic components in CAD to control ball routing across the water runoff-themed playfield
  • Building a sturdy, level enclosure using hand tools and power tools to tight tolerances so the ball rolled consistently
  • Iterating on flipper geometry and spring tension to achieve reliable, responsive gameplay
  • Integrating a tilt-angled playfield within the wooden chassis without racking or warping

Electronics & Sensors

The machine incorporated a custom electronics system built around an Arduino microcontroller. Motion sensors detected ball position and triggered scoring events, while relay logic controlled solenoids and LED feedback. Key work included:

  • Wiring and soldering all analog and digital components including sensors, buttons, and indicator LEDs
  • Debugging signal processing issues and tuning sensor thresholds through iterative bench testing
  • Designing the circuit layout to fit within the enclosure without interference

Outcomes

The finished machine was demonstrated at the Cornerstone of Engineering final showcase. It passed all performance criteria on first demo — ball routing, scoring, and sensor triggering all functioned as designed. The sustainability theme was well received, with the playfield artwork and banner clearly communicating the water runoff concept to visitors.
